data.title: Palm Oil Rises Amid Bargain Hunting — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil prices rise in early Asian trade amid bargain hunting. The recent appreciation of the ringgit may have weighed on overall demand for crude palm oil, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. A firmer ringgit tends to pressure crude palm oil prices as the commodity is traded in the Malaysian currency. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency's proposal to focus biofuel blending targets on large refiners dampens demand for vegetable oils, it adds. AmInvestment Bank pegs support for CPO futures at 4,381 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,568 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives December contract is 10 ringgit higher at 4,445 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Indonesia includes tax amnesty bill in 2025 priority legislation, lawmaker says
data.description:
The legislative body of Indonesia's parliament has included a tax amnesty bill as priority legislation this year, a lawmaker said on Friday, in another bid to generate state revenue from undeclared assets.

Southeast Asia's biggest economy carried out two tax amnesty programmes, in 2016 and 2022, which collectively uncovered more than $340 billion worth of unreported assets.

Taxpayers were offered lower-than-normal tax rates if they disclosed hidden assets, while authorities pledged to penalise those who did not participate.

The government at the time had promised not to hold further amnesties, but to improve tax compliance.

The tax amnesty bill had been on the priority list from the beginning and was not suddenly included, lawmaker Martin Manurung told Reuters, adding it would still require a wider parliamentary vote at a later date,

He provided no details of the proposed bill and referred Reuters to parliament's commission for finance affairs.

It was not immediately clear what the bill would entail and Indonesia's finance ministry did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

data.title: Taiwan Central Bank Leaves Door Open for Monetary-Policy Easing — Market Talk
data.description: Taiwan's central bank leaves the door open for monetary-policy easing, DBS Group Research's Ma Tieying says in commentary. The central bank's governor stated that domestic inflation is clearly trending downward, and that tying rate cuts strictly to inflation falling below 1.5% would be too rigid, the senior economist notes. The governor also said the "outlook for moderate economic growth" must be reassessed on a quarterly basis, citing risks from potential Section 232 tariffs from the U.S. and a slowdown in Taiwan's key information and communications technology sector. "These factors suggest the possibility of a rate cut within the next 12 months, with the earliest likely timing being December 2025," the economist adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0304 GMT - Taiwan's central bank leaves the door open for monetary-policy easing, DBS Group Research's Ma Tieying says in commentary. The central bank's governor stated that domestic inflation is clearly trending downward, and that tying rate cuts strictly to inflation falling below 1.5% would be too rigid, the senior economist notes. The governor also said the "outlook for moderate economic growth" must be reassessed on a quarterly basis, citing risks from potential Section 232 tariffs from the U.S. and a slowdown in Taiwan's key information and communications technology sector. "These factors suggest the possibility of a rate cut within the next 12 months, with the earliest likely timing being December 2025," the economist adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0257 GMT - South Korea is likely to reach a partial compromise with the U.S. on trade talks rather than face 25% or higher reciprocal auto tariffs, though the latter chances may rise if other economies accept investment deals on U.S. terms, says Citi's Korea economist Jin-Wook Kim. Quick finalization of Korea's $350 billion U.S. investment fund appears unlikely near term, given structural constraints, Kim says in a note. Washington could accept Korea's request for a smaller annual financing amount and pledges into key industries such as shipbuilding and batteries without binding terms. However, if talks fail, U.S. demand-dependent Korean manufacturing sectors like autos and household appliances may suffer, and Korean private companies' investment into the U.S. could stall, Kim cautions.(jason.chau@wsj.com)

0252 GMT - Indonesia's growth is expected to slow slightly to 4.9% in 2025 from 5.03% in 2024, before reaching 5.0% in 2026, says BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions, in a note. Growth headwinds include U.S. tariffs, subdued Chinese demand and President Prabowo Subianto's populist policies, which could weigh on consumer confidence, investment and the garment sector, BMI says. Limited fiscal room may constrain government stimulus, though Bank Indonesia's 125 bps of rate cuts, along with further cuts expected ahead, could provide some support, it says. Despite these challenges, Indonesia's low U.S. exposure and stable historical growth suggest the 5.0% growth forecast for 2026 is achievable, BMI adds. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0218 GMT - The Malaysian ringgit may trade in the 4.15-4.20 range against the U.S. dollar in the near term, as the Fed shifts decisively dovish, Kenanga economists say in a note. They expect two more 25bp cuts by the Fed this year, further pressuring an already weak dollar. Markets will watch for weaker labor data to support expectations of further Fed easing. Attention is on core PCE, where a stronger reading could dampen hopes for near-term cuts, they note. Investors will also monitor housing data for signs of slowdowns that could influence the pace of Fed easing. They reckon USD/MYR to finish 2025 at 4.08. USD/MYR faces resistance at 4.21 and support at 4.19 in the short term, they add. USD/MYR is 0.3% higher at 4.2080. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0213 GMT - The Monetary Authority of Singapore is likely to resume policy easing in October, after the central bank kept its policy band unchanged in July, say analysts at BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions, in a report. Singapore's labor market remains tight and wage growth has moderated, which further limits inflationary pressure, they say. BMI expects Singapore's 2025 core inflation to average 0.8%, below the midpoint of the MAS's 0.5%-1.5% range. Growth is also expected to slow sharply in 2H, the analysts say.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0054 GMT - USD/JPY's momentum bias appears firmly higher within its trading range, based on charts, StoneX's Matt Simpson says in commentary. The currency pair closed above its 200-day exponential moving average, while a strong directional rally can be seen on the 1-hour chart, the senior market analyst says. A rise by USD/JPY to the top of its range and a possible retest of the 200-day EMA, which was at 147.80, could be on the horizon, Simpson adds. USD/JPY is little changed at 148.05. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0041 GMT - Asian currencies consolidate against the dollar in early Asian trade, but may be supported by risk appetite. Markets have reassessed the Fed's stance, with traders taking a more positive view of the FOMC decision and guidance, NAB's Rodrigo Catril says in commentary. Fed Chair Powell's "meeting by meeting" guidance is perceived as prudent, the senior FX strategist says. Also, U.S. economic data released overnight "painted a picture" of resilience in the labor market and a rebound in business sentiment, Catril adds. USD/JPY edges 0.1% higher to 148.09; AUD/USD is little changed at 0.6609. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0031 GMT - Japan's August CPI data released earlier likely backs the case for the BOJ to resume monetary-policy tightening before year-end, Capital Economics' Abhijit Surya says in a note. "The Bank of Japan will probably look past the ongoing fall in headline inflation, given that underlying inflation is holding up reasonably well," the senior APAC economist says. Japan's inflation excluding fresh food and energy slowed only marginally to 3.3% in August from 3.4% in July, Surya notes. Capital Economics' base case remains that the BOJ will raise its policy rate by 25bps at the October meeting. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0019 GMT - Japanese stocks are higher after major U.S. stock indexes hit record highs overnight. A weaker yen is also giving a boost to the market. Electronics makers and trading houses are leading the gains. Lasertec is up 10% and Mitsubishi Corp. is 3.4% higher. USD/JPY is at 148.07, up from 147.49 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors will be focusing on the Bank of Japan's policy meeting decision due later Friday. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 1.0% at 45752.51. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0012 GMT - JGBs edge lower in price terms in early Tokyo trade after data released earlier showed Japan's consumer prices stayed well above the BOJ's 2% target in August, supporting prospects for a rate hike. However, investors may adopt wait-and-see mood ahead of the BOJ's monetary-policy decision due today. The BOJ is expected to leave its policy rate unchanged at 0.5%, three members of BofA Global Research say in a note. "The focus will be Governor Ueda's press conference, at which the market will look for clues on the potential for a rate hike at the October" meeting, the members say. The 10-year JGB yield is up 1 bp at 1.605%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2343 GMT - Japanese stocks may rise after major U.S. stock indexes hit record highs overnight. A weaker yen may also boost the market. Nikkei futures are up 0.8% at 45610 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 147.97, up from 147.49 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors will be focusing on the Bank of Japan's policy meeting outcome due later Friday. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 1.1% to 45303.43 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1947 GMT - Treasurys mostly sell off, keeping yields slightly on the green, and the dollar strengthens as markets contemplate higher long-term uncertainty. The Fed looks ready to keep cutting rates in coming meetings, but today's labor and activity data indicate it might not need to hurry. Fedspeak is likely to be closely monitored in coming days. New governor Stephen Miran, the only FOMC member to vote for a deeper, 50-basis point cut yesterday, will deliver a speech at The Economic Club of New York Monday. The 10-year rises 0.031 percentage point to 4.104% and the two-year adds 0.021 p.p. to 3.567%. The WSJ Dollar Index rises 0.4%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Palm tracks gains of rival oils, on track for weekly gains
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Friday, as the contract tracked gains in rival soyoil at Dalian and Chicago exchanges, and is set to book weekly gains.

The benchmark palm oil contract for December delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 22 Malaysian ringgit, or 0.5%, to 4,457 ringgit ($1,059.93) a metric ton, as of 0239 GMT.

The futures has gained 0.11% so far for the week.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) rose 0.82%, while its palm oil contract increased 0.21%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) gained 0.25%.

* Palm oil tracks the price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* Malaysia has raised its October crude palm oil reference price to a level that maintains the export duty range at 10%, a statement by the Malaysian Palm Oil Board showed on Friday.

* Brazilian farmers are expected to increase the country's soybean output in the new season, producing nearly 178 million metric tons, Brazilian crop agency Conab said on Thursday.

* According to cargo surveyor Intertek Testing Services, exports of Malaysian palm oil products for September 1-15 rose 2.6% compared to August 1-15, while according to independent inspection company AmSpec Agri Malaysia, they fell 0.1%.

* The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, weakened 0.26% against the dollar, making the commodity cheaper for buyers holding foreign currencies.

* Palm oil may retest support at 4,401 ringgit per metric ton, a break below could open the way toward the 4,342 ringgit-4,366 ringgit range, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said.

MARKET NEWS

* Asian shares are set to end a big central week in positive territory, buoyed by hopes of more monetary stimulus around the world, while the Nikkei notched a record aided by expectations the Bank of Japan would again skip a rate hike.

* Oil prices were little changed on Friday after settling lower in the previous session, the day after the U.S. Federal Reserve cut interest rates for the first time this year, due to worries about fuel demand in the United States.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

0600 UK Retail Sales MM, YY Aug

0600 UK Retail Sales Ex-Fuel MM Aug

0645 France Business Climate Mfg, Overall Sep

($1 = 4.2050 ringgit)

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 11 PM ET: Tech Deal, Rate Cut Push Stocks to Records | Trump's ...
data.description:
Tech Deal, Rate Cut Push Stocks to Records

Intel has best day since 1987 after Nvidia invests $5 billion.

----

Trump's Team Explores Government-Backed Manufacturing Boost

Discussions center on how to use money from a $550 billion fund to spur the construction of factories and other infrastructure in the U.S.

----

Japan Consumer Prices Rise, But at Slower Pace

Japan's consumer prices rose at a slower pace in August but remained well above the central bank's 2% target, backing the case for an interest-rate increase.

----

Treasury yields rise after Fed rate cut, with Powell in no 'sprint' to loosen policy

Investors in the bond market are adjusting their positioning in the aftermath of the Fed's shift in monetary policy.

----

U.K. Consumer Sentiment Falls Back

The mood of British consumers dampened a little this month, with expectations of a slowing economy and the potential for tax increases later this year threatening an even greater downturn.

----

Trump's Trade War Split Canada and Mexico. Now They Want a United Front.

America's top two trading partners are seeking a strategic partnership ahead of negotiations with the U.S. over tariffs.

----

Florida Orange Output Lowest Since the Great Depression

The amount of oranges grown in the state has fallen 32% from the previous year and 82% since the beginning of the decade, according to the USDA.

----

Trump Administration Asks Supreme Court to Allow Removal of Fed Governor Lisa Cook

A lower court blocked Cook's firing while she challenges the lawfulness of her termination.

----

U.S. Leading Indicators Continue to Show Weakening Economy

The U.S. economy faces a slowing economy amid growing concerns about the labor market, headwinds from tariffs and a weak manufacturing sector, according to a basket of economic indicators.

----

U.S. Jobless Claims Fell Last Week

The number of Americans who newly filed for unemployment benefits declined sharply last week, returning the figures to the previous trend after a large spike the week before.

----

South African Reserve Bank Maintains Main Repo Rate at 7%

The decision pauses the cutting cycle it began in September when the bank started to reduce rates from a 15-year high of 8.25%.

----

Bank of England Leaves Key Rate on Hold, Slows Quantitative Tightening

The U.K.'s central bank left its key rate at 4% as policymakers are growing increasingly worried about a pickup in inflation.

----

What Decides Where Mortgage Rates Go From Here

While the Federal Reserve has begun cutting rates again, there are many other factors that affect the cost of home loans.

----

The Fed Rolls Back Recession Risk

The central bank's quarter-point rate cut has eliminated the U.S. economy's worst-case scenarios.

----

Norges Bank Cuts Key Rate But Hints at Very Gradual Easing Going Forward

The central bank cut its rate to 4% and said further easing would likely be needed for economic growth, as policymakers expect one rate cut a year in the coming three years.

Inside Microsoft's Plans for the 'Most Advanced AI Data Center in the World'

DuPont Cuts Full-Year Sales Guidance to Reflect Divestiture, Spinoff

----

Darden Raises Sales Outlook, Warns Rising Costs Will Squeeze Profit

The owner of Olive Garden and LongHorn Steakhouse restaurants raised its full-year outlook but cautioned that higher food and labor costs will hurt earnings in the near term.

data.title: Indonesia Growth Likely to Slow This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Indonesia's growth is expected to slow slightly to 4.9% in 2025 from 5.03% in 2024, before reaching 5.0% in 2026, says BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions, in a note. Growth headwinds include U.S. tariffs, subdued Chinese demand and President Prabowo Subianto's populist policies, which could weigh on consumer confidence, investment and the garment sector, BMI says. Limited fiscal room may constrain government stimulus, though Bank Indonesia's 125 bps of rate cuts, along with further cuts expected ahead, could provide some support, it says. Despite these challenges, Indonesia's low U.S. exposure and stable historical growth suggest the 5.0% growth forecast for 2026 is achievable, BMI adds. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: US 10-Year Yield Holds Advance
data.description:
The yield on the 10-year US Treasury hovered near 4.11% on Friday after climbing almost 10 basis points over the past two sessions, as the Federal Reserve signaled a less dovish stance than markets expected.

Earlier this week, the Fed delivered a widely anticipated quarter-point cut and projected two more reductions this year, while flagging just one cut in 2026.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell characterized the move as a risk management step in response to labor market weakness, emphasizing there was no need to accelerate easing.

Data on Thursday also showed weekly jobless claims fell, reversing the prior week’s spike.

At the same time, the Fed maintained the pace of quantitative tightening despite the sharp drawdown in its overnight reverse repo facility since the last meeting.

data.title: India debt supply concern set to limit early decline in bond yields
data.description:
By Dharamraj Dhutia

Indian government bond yields may trend lower in opening deals on Friday amid the potential for inclusion in another global bond index, but any fall would be limited as debt supply remains a major concern.

The yield on the 10-year benchmark (IN063335G=CC) note is expected to move in the 6.48%-6.52% range till the auction, said a trader at a private bank. It closed at 6.5139% on Thursday.

Bloomberg Index Services is seeking views from investors on whether Indian government bonds should be included in its flagship global aggregate index, according to a notice sent to investors seen by Reuters.

The index provider included India in its emerging market local currency bond index from January 2025.

"Prima facie, this is a positive development as it would open doors for more hefty inflows, but after the initial short covering, the market would be focusing on immediate concern which is debt auction, where cutoffs would be the key driver," the trader said.

New Delhi will sell the benchmark 2035 bond worth 300 billion rupees ($3.40 billion) later in the day. The auction comes at a time when market remains concerned about skewness in demand-supply dynamics.

Traders also await the borrowing calendar for the second half of the fiscal year, which is likely to be published before the end of September.

In a round of consultations with multiple market participants earlier this month, the central bank received suggestions to reduce the share of ultra-long bonds and cut weekly auction sizes.

Meanwhile, hawkish commentary from the U.S. Federal Reserve weighed on sentiment, even as the central bank reduced interest rates and indicated more cuts.

The 10-year U.S. yield inched further higher from the key 4% mark.

RATES

India's overnight index swaps are expected to extend their rise from the previous session.

India's overnight index swap rates moved higher on Thursday, with the longer end witnessing heavy paying.

The one-year OIS rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) ended at 5.47%, while the two-year OIS rate (INR2YMIBROIS=CC) closed at 5.44%.

The liquid five-year OIS rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 3 bps to settle at 5.7175%.

KEY INDICATORS:

** Benchmark Brent crude futures were down 0.1% at $67.40 per barrel, after easing 0.8% in previous session

** Ten-year U.S. Treasury yield was at 4.1102%; two-year yield (US2YT=RR) at 3.5635%

** India to auction sovereign bonds worth 360 billion rupees

** RBI to set underwriting fees for sovereign bond auction worth 360 billion rupees

** RBI to conduct six-day variable rate repo auction for 1 trillion rupees

($1 = 88.2180 Indian rupees)

data.title: US court moves to support Elliott affiliate's bid for Citgo parent
data.description:
By Marianna Parraga

A U.S. judge allowed an affiliate of hedge fund Elliott Investment Management on Thursday to move towards completing its offer for Venezuela-owned Citgo Petroleum's parent through a court-ordered auction of shares, while restricting rival bidder Gold Reserve from doing the same.

This followed a separate judge in another U.S. court upholding the validity of Venezuelan state oil company PDVSA's 2020 bonds on Thursday.

Delaware Judge Leonard Stark instructed a court officer overseeing the auction of shares to terminate a stock purchase agreement (SPA) with a Gold Reserve subsidiary and sign a new one with Elliott's affiliate Amber Energy, Gold Reserve said in a release.

That came at the end of a 4-day sale hearing, where some of the 15 Venezuela-linked creditors lining up to cash proceeds from the auction for Citgo's parent asked him to move the auction forward by granting key agreements with frontrunner bidder Amber.

The creditors have resorted to U.S. courts seeking compensation for debt defaults and expropriations in Venezuela.

An SPA is a legally binding contract that outlines the terms for the sale and transfer of a company's shares. In an auction of shares, the SPA is a key step to allow a frontrunner to secure financial commitments and complete regulatory filings for the shares' transfer.

Gold Reserve's unit Dalinar Energy and Amber have been competing neck and neck in recent months for Citgo's parent, PDV Holding.

Because Amber's $5.9 billion offer includes a separate $2.1 billion agreement to pay holders of a defaulted Venezuelan bond, the Delaware court has recommended it as the best bid. Dalinar's offer did not include a similar provision.

Judge Stark has not made a final decision on the winner. But he also denied on Thursday a motion by Gold Reserve to disqualify Amber's bid, the miner said in a statement.

"The decision was delivered orally from the bench. Gold Reserve expects a written opinion and order to be issued shortly," it added.

A New York judge on Thursday confirmed the validity of defaulted Venezuelan bonds collateralized with Citgo equity, supporting the holders' claim and boosting Amber's bid.

data.title: Adani Power shares have 29% upside, Morgan Stanley says citing turnaround
data.description:
Shares of https://www.cnbctv18.com/market/stocks/adani-power-share-price/AP11/?utm_source=liveblog&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=earningsAdani Power Ltd. are in focus on Friday, September 19, after brokerage firm Morgan Stanley initiated coverage on the stock, projecting an upside of 29% from its previous closing price.

Morgan Stanley has initiated coverage on the stock, with an "overweight" rating and a price target of ₹818 per share. The stock ended the previous session at ₹633.5 apiece.

The brokerage said Adani power is a good illustration of a turnaround in India's corporate history, with resolution on most regulatory issues and multiple value-accretive acquisitions.

Morgan Stanley said the company will deliver strong earnings growth, driven by the timely completion of projects and power purchase agreement (PPA) wins in the medium-term.

It also forecast that the company's capacity and earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) will rise 2.5 times and 3 times by the financial year 2033, respectively.

New coal Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) should improve investor confidence in earnings power, Morgan Stanley said.

All five analysts that have coverage on shares of Adani Power have "buy" recommendations on the stock.

Adani Group stocks, including Adani Power are also in focus after market regulator Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) gave a clean chit to the Group and its top executives on some allegations made by short seller Hindenburg Research, as it found no violation of laws, fraudulent practices, market manipulation or insider trading.

Shares of Adani Power ended the previous session 0.5% up. The stock has gained 21.6% in the last six months.

data.title: Offshore Yuan Continues to Fall, Yet Poised for Weekly Gain
data.description:
The offshore yuan depreciated past 7.11 per dollar on Friday, extending its decline from the previous session amid a stronger greenback.

The US dollar firmed after the Federal Reserve signaled it would adopt a cautious approach to easing monetary policy, following a 25 basis point rate cut announced on Wednesday.

While the yuan faced downward pressure from the strengthening dollar, its losses were partially offset by supportive developments out of Hong Kong.

In a move aimed at deepening the renminbi's role in international markets, Hong Kong announced plans to expand the use of the Chinese currency by issuing additional yuan-denominated bonds and settling a portion of government expenditures in renminbi.

Looking ahead, investors are turning their attention to the release of China’s loan prime rates over the weekend.

Despite back-to-back declines, the yuan is on track to notch a weekly gain.

** Analysts say co's guided increase in FY26 EBIT from Australia faces risks from upcoming wage discussions in Victoria and Queensland

** Note 'highly uncertain' prospects for co's 52.8% owned French care provider Santé due to sustained upward wage pressure and small increases in reimbursement

** Say despite a 9% growth in FY25 EBIT from UK business, ceased capex growth for UK site Elysium, along with persistent occupancy problems and cost pressures entails a longer recovery period for Elysium

** Currently, shares trading flat, falling 1.85% over the week to A$32.950

** YTD, stock down 4.6%

data.title: GK Energy IPO: GMP surges 24% as bidding opens; check price band, other details
data.description:
The ₹464.26 crore initial public offering (IPO) of Pune-based GK Energy is opening for subscription on Friday, September 19, and the three-day bidding process will remain open until September 23.

The company, which specialises in engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) for renewable energy projects, will sell its shares in a fixed price band of ₹145-153, with a lot size of 98 shares.

Investor appetite is evident in the grey market, where the company's shares are commanding a premium of 24% above the IPO price, signalling expectations of a healthy listing.

GMP is an unofficial price indicator in the grey market and may not always reflect the actual listing price on the stock exchange.

GK Energy anchor book

Ahead of the IPO opening, the company raised ₹139 crore from anchor investors including Pinebridge, Prashant Jain's 3P India Equity Fund. The company allotted 91.03 lakh shares at the upper price band to marquee domestic and global institutions.

Other big names that came in through the anchor book include HSBC Flexicap Fund, Citigroup Global Markets Mauritius, Motilal Oswal Large Cap Fund, Bandhan Small Cap Fund, 1M, Edelweiss Equity Savings Fund, among others.

GK Energy's IPO comprises a fresh issue of ₹400 crore and an offer for sale of ₹64.26 crore by promoters.

GK Energy IPO objective

The net proceeds from the IPO will primarily be used to meet long-term working capital requirements, estimated at around ₹322 crore. A portion of the funds will also be allocated for general corporate purposes.

GK Energy primarily operates as a focused EPC provider of solar-powered agricultural water pump systems. Its sales are divided into two main categories: direct-to-beneficiary sales and sales to other customers.

The company, one of the largest players in the government's PM-Kusum solar water pump scheme, has installed more than 62,000 solar-powered pumps across five states and reported revenues of over ₹1,095 crore in FY25, up from ₹285 crore in FY23. Profits also grew sharply to ₹133 crore in FY25.

IIFL Capital Services, and HDFC Bank are managing the GK Energy IPO as merchant bankers.

The tentative date for allotment of shares is September 24, while the tentative listing date, when the shares are expected to start trading on the stock exchange, is September 26, 2025.

data.title: ASX-listed Resmed rises; Citi lifts PT on margin expansion prospect
data.description:
** ASX-listed Resmed rises as much as 2.4% to A$41.910, highest since September 10

** Stock set for second straight week of consecutive gains, having risen 1.8% so far this week

** Citi raises PT to A$51 from A$49

** Expects healthcare tech co will expand margins further to 63% in FY27 from 60% in FY25 helped by its AirSense 11, which is used to treat sleep apnea

** Sees concerns to exist around drugs like Ozempic helping people beat obesity, a major cause of sleep apnea; fewer obese individuals could mean fewer patients needing co's devices

** Believes anecdotes suggest better sleep after Ozempic use but only small portion of market currently treated; says will take years before it becomes real threat

** Stock rated "buy" on avg; median PT is A$48.30, per data compiled by LSEG

** RMD gains ~13% YTD

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Poised for Third Straight Weekly Gains
data.description:
Hong Kong shares rose 68 points or 0.3% to 26,617 on Friday morning trade, rebounding from a 1.4% slump in the previous session as bargain hunters stepped in.

Record highs for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq overnight boosted sentiment, a day after the Fed cut rates for the first time in nine months.

Traders also looked ahead to a call between U.S. President Trump and Xi Jinping after a deal that would place TikTok under U.S. ownership.

For the week, the Hang Seng is on track for the third consecutive gain, up around 0.8% so far, supported by optimism over China’s progress in AI, semiconductors, and drug innovation.

Still, upside was limited by a pullback in mainland equities from decade highs after China's central bank signaled no urgency to ease monetary policy, fueling bets that fresh stimulus may be delayed until 2026.

Among individual movers, Weimob jumped 12% after a share sale for tech upgrades, while SMIC gained 3.9%, Anta Sports 3.6%, Galaxy Entertainment 3.2%, and Trip.com 2.1%.

data.title: China Stocks Struggle for Direction
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite slipped 0.1% to around 3,828 while the Shenzhen Component added 0.5% to 13,140 on Friday, with mainland shares lacking clear direction as investors searched for fresh catalysts.

On Thursday, Chinese stocks retreated as traders locked in profits after the Shanghai Index hit a ten-year high.

Sentiment was also pressured by the absence of new policy support, with the People’s Bank of China keeping a key rate unchanged and showing little urgency to ease further.

Meanwhile, the US Federal Reserve cut rates this week and signaled more reductions, potentially giving the PBOC space to act.

Among notable movers, Foxconn Industrial gained 5.1%, Luxshare Precision rose 4.7% and Wuxi Lead Intelligent advanced 4.3%, while Zhejiang Sanhua (-6.8%), Wolong Electric (-7.9%) and Cambricon Technologies (-1.7%) posted steep losses.

data.title: Aussie Dollar Declines for 3rd Session
data.description:
The Australian dollar slipped to around $0.660 on Friday, its third straight decline to pull back from an eleven-month peak reached mid-week.

The Aussie is also set for its first weekly loss in four weeks, pressured by a weaker-than-expected August jobs report that showed a surprise drop in employment, reinforcing bets of an RBA rate cut later this year.

Markets currently price only a 20% chance of a September move and a 70% chance in November.

The Q3 inflation print due in October will be pivotal, with the central bank noting that monthly figures are less reliable.

Investors also await next week’s PMI data to gauge the country’s economic health.

Adding pressure, the US dollar gained support from a less dovish policy stance from the Federal Reserve.

Chair Powell framed the quarter-point cut as a risk-management step amid a softening labor market, stressing there was no need to rush easing.

The greenback also found support as new jobless claims fell, reversing the prior week’s spike.

data.title: New Zealand Dollar Holds Near 2-Week low
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar hovered around $0.597 on Friday after sliding more than 1% in the previous session to a near two-week low, as a sharper-than-expected economic downturn reinforced bets of more rate cuts by the Reserve Bank.

GDP decreased 0.9% in the June quarter, worse than the forecasted 0.3% fall, following a revised 0.9% growth in the previous quarter, mainly dragged by weakness in the construction and manufacturing sectors, alongside softer exports.

Markets are now fully priced for a 25bps rate cut in October, while assigning about a 25% chance of a larger 50bps move.

They also imply 71bps of easing ahead, up from 50bps at the start of the week.

Separately, data out today showed New Zealand’s trade deficit narrowed to NZ$1.2 billion in August from NZ$2.3 billion in the same month last year, though still wider than market expectations of NZ$0.7 billion.

For the week, the kiwi is on track for a loss of more than 1%.

data.title: China's Genfleet Therapeutics soars in Hong Kong debut
data.description:
** Shares of Genfleet Therapeutics (Shanghai) Inc (2595.HK) trade as high as HK$50.2 in Hong Kong debut, up 146.2% from their offer price of HK$20.39 apiece

** Stock later trims gain to trade at HK$44.5, still up 118.2% from the offer price with 25.26 million shares worth HK$1.14 billion changing hands

** The tenth most active traded shares by turnover in early trade

** The Chinese innovative drugs developer and tumor treatment solutions provider offered 89.24 million shares, including overallotment option, for sale in global offering, raising HK$1.82 billion ($233.97 million) for development of core product and product candidates in the pipeline

** Says subscription level of Hong Kong public offering is 2,662.79 times, and international offering portion is 39.05 times

** Hang Seng Biotech Index HSI:HSHKBIO slips 0.5%, while benchmark Hang Seng Index adds 0.2%

($1 = 7.7788 Hong Kong dollars)

data.title: Thai king approves PM Anutin's cabinet line-up, Royal Gazette shows
data.description:
Thailand's King Maha Vajiralongkorn has endorsed Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's new cabinet, according to a notice in the Royal Gazette.

The endorsement formally enables the cabinet to take an oath before the king ahead of a policy statement to parliament, the final step before ministers can assume their duties.

data.title: British Business - September 19
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the business pages of British newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

The Times

- British food delivery firm Deliveroo said founder and CEO Will Shu will step down once its takeover by U.S. rival DoorDash takes effect.

- Octopus Energy said it would spin its technology arm Kraken Technologies off and named Tim Wan as the newly separated company's chief financial officer, as Britain largest electricity supplier focuses on its core operations.

The Guardian

- The UK is preparing to recognise the state of Palestine as early as Friday, after Israel failed to meet conditions that would have postponed the historic step, including a ceasefire in Gaza.

The Telegraph

- U.S. President Donald Trump has urged Keir Starmer to consider calling in the military to stop small boat crossings uring a joint press conference at the British Prime Minister's country residence.

- Webasto, a British manufacturer that supplies glass roofs to Jaguar Land Rover, has been forced to halt factory operations and begin laying off staff as the impact of a cyber attack grows.

Sky News

- A former boss of one of Britain's oldest business groups has been banned from holding company directorships for 11 years after abusing a state-backed emergency loan scheme for businesses struggling during the COVID pandemic.

- A former boss of Centrica , Iain Conn, the owner of British Gas, is among the candidates being considered to chair IP Group , the early-stage science investor.

Thai Union Group Raised to Hold From Sell by Thanachart Securities >TU.TH
